title: EOE condones rape Of course not but
display_title: EOE condones rape Of course not but
wikitext:
[]: EOE condones rape? Of course not, but...             Quote Post by SAWgunner » Fri Jul 15, 2011 8:06 am First of all, I want to say that I don't think it does. The reason I ask is that, when I showed End of Evangelion to a friend, he was downcast and appalled the whole night afterward. While listing what he didn't like about it (in a nutshell, it made him feel really bad for no specific reason), one of his reasons was that it "condoned rape". I never saw anything in EoE that could possibly be construed as condoning rape, but is there perhaps something I missed? If not, then what would possibly cause one to draw such a conclusion?

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Fri Jul 15, 2011 8:17 am ...

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Fri Jul 15, 2011 8:19 am That sounds pretty bogus.

[]:             Quote Post by SAWgunner » Fri Jul 15, 2011 8:33 am He read the manga before watching EoE because I didn't own the show at the time; but the manga doesn't diverge that much from the show! Shinji's Solo Performance wouldn't be a great example either, since it was portrayed negatively. I have no idea where he's coming from. Then again, this is the same guy who liked the characters in Rebuild a little more because "they're not as one-dimensional". Go figure. I also don't understand why people don't like EoE in the first place but I digress.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Fri Jul 15, 2011 8:45 am SAWgunner wrote: Then again, this is the same guy who liked the characters in Rebuild a little more because "they're not as one-dimensional".

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Fri Jul 15, 2011 9:03 am SAWgunner wrote: Then again, this is the same guy who liked the characters in Rebuild a little more because "they're not as one-dimensional". Go figure.

[]:             Quote Post by SAWgunner » Fri Jul 15, 2011 9:07 am You won't find one. I basically called him out and he couldn't name an example. I still wonder where he got that idea from though, which is why I started this convo. Not to mention that I like to second-guess myself, keeps me honest.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Fri Jul 15, 2011 9:11 am He probably felt raped in his head. That happened to me watching it too. And I kinda hated it for two days because of it, along with the entire TV series.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Fri Jul 15, 2011 9:27 am The End of Evangelion --) Mindfuck --) Mindfucked by movie you dislike --) "Fuck you movie!" --) Movie condones rape ---) LIKE A BOSS

[]:             Quote Post by Jornophelanthas » Fri Jul 15, 2011 11:18 am @SAWgunner: My interpretation of your friend's reactions (as presented by you) is that he confuses portrayal and approval by the creators of EoE. After all, EoE shows us: - a masturbation scene over the comatose body of a teenage girl; - another teenage girl appearing naked in literally every scene she's in, save one; - an adult woman kissing a clinically depressed teenage boy on the lips; - an adult man groping and fondling a naked teenage girl right through her skin, while she looks on indifferently (at first); - various people being turned to liquid after being (sexually) assaulted by an image from their own (sexual) fantasies; - various sexual positions imagery during the latter half of the movie, including the infamous strangulation scene at the end. I think your friend confuses the creators portraying such explicit material with their approval of it, because in his opinion such sexual material should never be shown in media at all. This is, in my opinion, a common (mis)conception in American media culture. Because US media tend to be very puritan in their portrayal of sexual imagery, and the American public often overreacts to being exposed to sexual items unexpectedly. Furthermore, any sexually tinted actions (fictional or otherwise) involving minors, is commonly considered to be rape or sexual assault in this prevailing cultural opinion, especially if one of the parties acts without the explicit approval of the other. Three examples: - The Janet Jackson wardrobe malfunction during the Superbowl live broadcast caused significant media backlash, resulting in both celebrities and media stations involved to apologize profusely in order to avoid being considered immoral by the public; - Pictures of breastfeeding woman are considered inappropriate in American media culture because any images of the female nipple are considered to be overtly sexual, even in this setting. - Even the use of words for genitalia on US television is strictly regulated, with certain words only being permitted at certain hours of the day, and some not even allowed at all. (Ironically, this puritanism does not extend to portrayals of violence.)

[]:             Quote Post by Someone » Fri Jul 15, 2011 11:45 am I think your friend ment mindrape.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Fri Jul 15, 2011 12:20 pm Someone wrote: I think your friend ment mindrape.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Fri Jul 15, 2011 3:25 pm Your friend is probably trolling you.

[]:             Quote Post by merri » Fri Jul 15, 2011 5:53 pm It never really condones rape, and with the exception of the sounds made by Misato and Kaji that one night, there is no real "sex" at all (but a lot of sexual imagery, especially in EoE). The closest thing to rape is when the harpies all attack Asuka. But mind-fuck, yeah. When I watched the original episodes 25 and 26, I went "What the hell?". Also, mind rape usually refers to what happened to Asuka at the end of the series, not really the viewer's mind after watching the series.

[]:             Quote Post by Korgan » Sat Jul 16, 2011 6:25 am There's a flash of Misato and Kaji having sex onscreen in an alley in ep. 15.

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Sat Jul 16, 2011 9:11 am I thought that was just her puking.

[]:             Quote Post by Blackmagebrad » Sat Jul 16, 2011 10:32 am Korgan wrote: There's a flash of Misato and Kaji having sex onscreen in an alley in ep. 15.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sat Jul 16, 2011 12:39 pm Stryker wrote: I thought that was just her puking.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sat Jul 16, 2011 12:50 pm Stryker wrote: I thought that was just her puking.

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Sat Jul 16, 2011 1:05 pm Blackmagebrad wrote: It's likely just a bias towards the first material this person viewed, I.E. the manga.
